mnemonics: Myerson's Optimal Auction: Maximizing Revenue by Valuing Virtual Values (MV^2)
Revenue Equivalence: Different Rules, Same Revenue (DRSR)
Ironing Out the Kinks: Fixing Non-Monotonic Virtual Valuations (FIX-M)
keyPoints: Myerson's Lemma states that the optimal auction allocates the item to the bidder with the highest virtual value.

The Revenue Equivalence Theorem establishes that any auction format satisfying certain conditions (e.g., allocating to the highest bidder, giving zero payoff to the lowest type) yields the same expected revenue.
The Ironing Procedure is a technique used to handle cases where the virtual valuation function is not monotone, ensuring the optimal mechanism is incentive-compatible.
commonMistakes: Confusing virtual value with actual value; virtual value incorporates the distribution of valuations and is key for revenue maximization.
Assuming Myerson's auction is always efficient; it maximizes revenue, which may not align with allocating the item to the bidder who values it most (if their virtual value is not the highest).
Applying the revenue equivalence theorem without verifying its assumptions (e.g., that the auction allocates to the highest-value bidder and that the lowest type has zero expected surplus).
